title: Make It Stick: The Science of Successful Learning
author: Peter C. Brown, Henry L. Roediger III, Mark A. McDaniel
excerpt: A sweeping reassessment of how learning actually works, grounded in cognitive science and debunking popular myths—showing that the most effective learning strategies are often counterintuitive, requiring effort, struggle, and the willingness to forget.
keyIdeas: Retrieval practice is far more effective than re-reading or review
Spacing out learning sessions produces stronger long-term retention
Interleaving different topics strengthens the ability to discriminate between concepts
Desirable difficulties make learning harder in the short term but stronger in the long term
keyTakeaways: Replace re-reading and highlighting with active recall and self-testing
Space your study sessions over time rather than cramming
Mix up different topics and problem types rather than blocking them
Embrace struggle—if learning feels easy, you're probably not learning much
whoShouldRead: Students and lifelong learners who want to learn more effectively
Educators and instructional designers seeking evidence-based teaching methods
Professionals who need to retain and apply complex information
Anyone who has ever felt they studied hard but didn't retain what they learned
whoShouldSkip: Readers looking for quick, effortless learning hacks
Those unwilling to challenge their existing study habits
